nativeRange.habitat: Rapidly flowing creeks and rivers with sandy bottoms; also found in slower reaches of large river systems

diet.notes: Primarily feeds on insect larvae, frozen/freeze-dried bloodworms, blackworms, brine shrimp, and appropriately sized sinking pellets. Avoid relying solely on flake foods. Nocturnal feeder — feed after lights out.

avoidWith: other Black Ghost Knifefish (territorial conspecific aggression)
fast nippy fish like some rainbowfish

summary: A striking 18-inch South American oddball that uses weak electric fields to navigate. Needs a large, heavily-decorated tank with caves and dim lighting; generally peaceful with fish too large to eat.
careNotes: The Black Ghost Knifefish is a nocturnal, electroreceptive fish that uses weak electrical pulses for navigation and communication (poses no danger to humans or tankmates). Juveniles can start in a 55-gallon (208 L) tank but adults require 120–180 gallons (450–680 L) for long-term health. Provide plenty of hiding places — caves, PVC pipes, and driftwood — as this species is shy and stress-prone without shelter. Keep lighting subdued; floating plants help diffuse light. Peaceful toward most tankmates but becomes predatory toward small fish (under ~6 cm) as it matures, and is highly territorial toward conspecifics. Do not house two Black Ghost Knifefish together unless in a very large (200+ gallon) tank with visual barriers. Feed after lights out. Sensitive to water quality; maintain good filtration and perform regular water changes. Note on water parameters: Aquarium Co-Op cites pH 6.8–7.8 while FishBase records 6.0–8.0; a conservative middle range of 6.5–7.5 is used here. Adult size: Aquarium Co-Op cites 35–45 cm; FishBase records maximum 50 cm — both figures are reflected in the adultSizeCm range. Noteworthy trait: this species is capable of jumping from open tanks; a tight-fitting lid is strongly recommended.
breedingNotes: Breeding in captivity is extremely rare and not documented under typical home aquarium conditions. No reliable captive breeding protocol is established; wild-caught individuals make up the vast majority of the trade.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked sizing and water parameters. FishBase records max size 50 cm (vs. 35–45 cm from primary) and pH 6.0–8.0 (vs. 6.8–7.8 from primary). Conservative values adopted; discrepancies noted in careNotes. FishBase was unreachable directly; data retrieved via web search cache.

fish.breedingNotes: Breeding in captivity is extremely rare and not reliably documented. No established home aquarium breeding protocol exists.
